text.div:
Generated Narrative: StructureDefinition EHDSMedicationPrescriptionObligations

NameFlagsCard.TypeDescription & Constraints
EHDSMedicationPrescriptionObligations 0..*EHDSMedicationPrescriptionMedication prescription model obligations

Elements defined in Ancestors:header, header, presentedForm, prescriptionItem
header
subject O1..1EHDSPatientThe person for whom the medication is prescribed/ordered. [Used for searching]
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
identifier O1..*IdentifierBusiness identifier(s) for the prescription. [Used for searching]
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
author[x] O1..*EHDSHealthProfessional, EHDSOrganisationThe prescriber, the person who made the prescription, and who takes the responsibility for the treatment. [Used for searching]
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
date O1..1dateTimeTime of issuing (signing) the prescription by health care professional. [Used for searching]
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
status O1..1CodeableConceptStatus of authorisation for dispensing the prescription items. This should not be the status of treatment. For a multiple-item prescription, this conveys the aggregate status of the entire prescription. In case of a single-item prescription, prescriptionItem.status shall be the same as EHDSMedicationPrescription.header.status. [Used for searching]
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
presentedForm O0..*EHDSAttachmentEntire prescription as issued. Various formats could be provided, PDF format is recommended.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
prescriptionItem O1..*BasePrescription line for one medication. In many countries, only one item is allowed and there shall be no expectation to be able to manage multiple items. In case multiple medications are allowed, all items need to be authored together.
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
identifier O1..*IdentifierIdentifier for a single item on prescription, if exists. In case of single-item prescription, this identifier is typically the same as prescription identifier. [Used for searching]
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
status O1..1CodeableConceptStatus of a single item of a multi-item prescription. In case of single-item prescriptions, the status of prescription has the same meaning as the status of the item. [Used for searching]
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
medication O1..1EHDSMedicationPrescribed product, branded, generic, virtual, extemporal, etc
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
quantityPrescribed O0..1QuantityOverall quantity of prescribed product (e.g number of packages or number of tablets).
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
dosageInstructions O1..1EHDSDosageDosage and administration instructions
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
validityPeriod O0..1PeriodThe period over which the prescription is considered valid and dispensable. validityPeriod.start is, if missing, is assumed to be the date of issuing the prescription.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
substitution O0..1BaseWhether and which type of substitution is allowed for this prescription. The legislation in the country of dispense applies, e.g. if this information is missing.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
note O0..1stringAdditional information or comments, e.g. message to the dispenser
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer

Documentation for this format

name: EHDSMedicationPrescriptionObligations
title: Medication prescription model obligations
description: Obligations for the logical model for medication prescription body for prescribing systems.

element.short: Medication prescription model obligations
element.definition: Obligations for the logical model for medication prescription body for prescribing systems.

element.short: Prescription header
element.definition: Prescription header

element.short: The person for whom the medication is prescribed/ordered. [Used for searching]
element.definition: The person for whom the medication is prescribed/ordered. [Used for searching]

extension.valueMarkdown: For a single-item prescription these identifiers SHALL match prescriptionItem.identifier.

element.short: Business identifier(s) for the prescription. [Used for searching]
element.definition: Business identifier(s) for the prescription. [Used for searching]

extension.valueMarkdown: System SHALL support at least EHDSHealthProfessional data type for the element.

element.short: The prescriber, the person who made the prescription, and who takes the responsibility for the treatment. [Used for searching]
element.definition: The prescriber, the person who made the prescription, and who takes the responsibility for the treatment. [Used for searching]

element.short: Time of issuing (signing) the prescription by health care professional. [Used for searching]
element.definition: Time of issuing (signing) the prescription by health care professional. [Used for searching]

element.short: Status of authorisation for dispensing the prescription items. This should not be the status of treatment. For a multiple-item prescription, this conveys the aggregate status of the entire prescription. In case of a single-item prescription, prescriptionItem.status shall be the same as EHDSMedicationPrescription.header.status. [Used for searching]
element.definition: Status of authorisation for dispensing the prescription items. This should not be the status of treatment. For a multiple-item prescription, this conveys the aggregate status of the entire prescription. In case of a single-item prescription, prescriptionItem.status shall be the same as EHDSMedicationPrescription.header.status. [Used for searching]

element.short: Source of information (e.g. patient, patient's proxy). Used in case the patient has authored the information or the author has submitted patient-stated information.
element.definition: Source of information (e.g. patient, patient's proxy). Used in case the patient has authored the information or the author has submitted patient-stated information.

element.short: Language in which the resource is written. Language is expressed by the IETF language tag.
element.definition: Language in which the resource is written. Language is expressed by the IETF language tag.

element.short: Reason for the current status of prescription, for example the reason why the prescription was made invalid or changed from a previous state.
element.definition: Reason for the current status of prescription, for example the reason why the prescription was made invalid or changed from a previous state.

extension.valueMarkdown: A system making prescriptions available shall provide a rendered, human-readable version of the prescription,

element.short: Entire prescription as issued. Various formats could be provided, PDF format is recommended.
element.definition: Entire prescription as issued. Various formats could be provided, PDF format is recommended.

element.short: Prescription line for one medication. In many countries, only one item is allowed and there shall be no expectation to be able to manage multiple items. In case multiple medications are allowed, all items need to be authored together.
element.definition: Prescription line for one medication. In many countries, only one item is allowed and there shall be no expectation to be able to manage multiple items. In case multiple medications are allowed, all items need to be authored together.

extension.valueMarkdown: For a single-item prescription these identifiers SHALL match .identifier.

element.short: Identifier for a single item on prescription, if exists. In case of single-item prescription, this identifier is typically the same as prescription identifier. [Used for searching]
element.definition: Identifier for a single item on prescription, if exists. In case of single-item prescription, this identifier is typically the same as prescription identifier. [Used for searching]

element.short: Status of a single item of a multi-item prescription. In case of single-item prescriptions, the status of prescription has the same meaning as the status of the item. [Used for searching]
element.definition: Status of a single item of a multi-item prescription. In case of single-item prescriptions, the status of prescription has the same meaning as the status of the item. [Used for searching]

element.short: Reason for the current status of prescription, for example the reason why the prescription was made invalid or why the prescription was changed from previous
element.definition: Reason for the current status of prescription, for example the reason why the prescription was made invalid or why the prescription was changed from previous

element.short: Prescribed product, branded, generic, virtual, extemporal, etc
element.definition: Prescribed product, branded, generic, virtual, extemporal, etc

element.short: Reason for the prescription (typically diagnosis, or a procedure)
element.definition: Reason for the prescription (typically diagnosis, or a procedure)

element.short: Intent of the prescription - prophylaxis, treatment, anesthesia, etc
element.definition: Intent of the prescription - prophylaxis, treatment, anesthesia, etc

element.short: Period over which the medication is to be taken (in case of multiple dosage schemes, this would be the overall period of all dosages.)
element.definition: Period over which the medication is to be taken (in case of multiple dosage schemes, this would be the overall period of all dosages.)

element.short: Overall quantity of prescribed product (e.g number of packages or number of tablets).
element.definition: Overall quantity of prescribed product (e.g number of packages or number of tablets).

element.short: Dosage and administration instructions
element.definition: Dosage and administration instructions

element.short: The period over which the prescription is considered valid and dispensable. validityPeriod.start is, if missing, is assumed to be the date of issuing the prescription.
element.definition: The period over which the prescription is considered valid and dispensable. validityPeriod.start is, if missing, is assumed to be the date of issuing the prescription.

extension.valueMarkdown: System SHALL support at least boolean data type for the element.

element.short: Whether and which type of substitution is allowed for this prescription. The legislation in the country of dispense applies, e.g. if this information is missing.
element.definition: Whether and which type of substitution is allowed for this prescription. The legislation in the country of dispense applies, e.g. if this information is missing.

element.short: Whether and to what extent substitution is allowed.
element.definition: Whether and to what extent substitution is allowed.

element.short: Reason for the substitution requirement (e.g. Biological product, Patient allergic to an excipient in alternative products, etc)
element.definition: Reason for the substitution requirement (e.g. Biological product, Patient allergic to an excipient in alternative products, etc)

element.short: Number of refills authorised - how many times the prescription item can be dispensed in addition to the original dispense. The default value is 0 (no repeats).
element.definition: Number of refills authorised - how many times the prescription item can be dispensed in addition to the original dispense. The default value is 0 (no repeats).

element.short: Minimum dispense interval. If a prescription allows for repeated dispensations, the interval between dispensations shall be stated here.
element.definition: Minimum dispense interval. If a prescription allows for repeated dispensations, the interval between dispensations shall be stated here.

element.short: Indicates that the prescriber has knowingly prescribed the medication for an indication, age group, dosage, or route of administration that is not approved by the regulatory agencies and is not mentioned in the prescribing information for the drug
element.definition: Indicates that the prescriber has knowingly prescribed the medication for an indication, age group, dosage, or route of administration that is not approved by the regulatory agencies and is not mentioned in the prescribing information for the drug

element.short: Indicates off-label use. Must be 'true' when .reason is provided.
element.definition: Indicates off-label use. Must be 'true' when .reason is provided.

element.short: Reason or related clarification for off-label use
element.definition: Reason or related clarification for off-label use

element.short: Additional information or comments, e.g. message to the dispenser
element.definition: Additional information or comments, e.g. message to the dispenser

element.short: Medication prescription model obligations
element.definition: Obligations for the logical model for medication prescription body for prescribing systems.

extension.valueMarkdown: For a single-item prescription these identifiers SHALL match prescriptionItem.identifier.
System SHALL support at least EHDSHealthProfessional data type for the element.
A system making prescriptions available shall provide a rendered, human-readable version of the prescription,
For a single-item prescription these identifiers SHALL match .identifier.
System SHALL support at least boolean data type for the element.
